text:
Olympic icon Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce amusingly outclassed her competitors during a parents' race at sports day. The eight-timeOlympicmedallist demonstrated her fierce competitive spirit by ruthlessly destroying the other mums. Fraser-Pryce boasts two Olympic gold medals in the 100m from the 2008 Beijing games and London 2012, along with a third gold in the 4x100m relay from Tokyo 2020.

Social media footage showed the Jamaican sprinter taking the event seriously, even warming up on the start line of the uneven grass track. Fraser-Pryce was seen stretching her legs with a high-knees run while the other parents congregated nearby. A second video provided an aerial view of the race, with the 38-year-old starting in the middle lane. Her reaction time proved to be as sharp as ever, as she was the first off the line in the 100m. Just a few metres into the race, she had already established a clear lead and continued to distance herself from the other parents.

As the video progressed, Fraser-Pryce crossed the finish line around four seconds faster than the other parents, so fast that the video struggled to keep her in frame. Fraser-Pryce is the third fastest woman in history, having set a personal best of 10.60, and is widely regarded as the greatest female sprinter of all time, having won more sprint titles than any other woman in history.

Enthralled fans flocked to social media to gush over the sensational clip, with one user exclaiming: "Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce DEMOLISHING everyone in a parents' race at her son's school." Another marvelled: "Can't tell if this clip has been sped up, but Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce just might go 10.6 again."

A third chimed in with amusement: "She even 'warmed up' lol". One observer quipped humorously: "She could've held back a little bit!" while another remarked: "Couldn't even keep her in the frame."

8x Olympic medalist ????????????Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce DEMOLISHING everyone in a parents race at her son's school ??????(?????? _suediamond/IG)pic.twitter.com/EVgpmBQJa5

At her seven-year-old son Zyon's sports day, Fraser-Pryce's presence was felt with undeniable pride as the sprint legend watched young Zyon claim victories in two of his competitions – proof that athletic prowess runs in the family.

Fraser-Pryce, famed as the third fastest woman in history, had demonstrated her unmatched speed during a parents' race at a previous sports day, when she notably put a boastful parent firmly in their place in 2023. Despite withdrawing from the 100m semi-final at Paris 2024 due to an unfortunate injury, the track icon has proclaimed she has "unfinished business" and plans to make a powerful comeback.

title: Olympic icon mum bests all other parents in son's sports day race | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fasika Zelealem, Cameron Winstanley
description: Shelly-Ann Fraser-Pryce is an eight-time Olympic medallist, and her competitive edge was clear for all to see at her son Zyon's sports day.
